commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor/exact-path proof and maintainer judgment on linked-issue scope, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes Telegram dispatch code and colocated tests; no concrete security or supply-chain concern was found. (sha=a5505616a438b61f92700672083b4f1f9feac71e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram dispatch fallback/status handling so a late dispatch error does not send the generic failure message after a final reply was delivered, suppressed, or queued, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +43. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the Telegram half from source inspection: current main sends the generic fallback whenever `dispatchError` is set, even after delivery state is already marked delivered. I did not run a live current-main reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor/exact-path proof and maintainer judgment on linked-issue scope, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix #87299: [Bug]: Spurious "Something went wrong" and Codex app-server failures in large Telegram direct sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram direct-session turns could show a successful final answer and then append the generic `Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again.` fallback when a later dispatch/cleanup error was recorded.
- Solution: Gate the visible failure fallback on the delivery summary and queued-final result, so the fallback is sent only when no final response was delivered, suppressed, or queued.
- What changed: Updated `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` and its regression coverage in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.
- What did NOT change: No Telegram setup/auth policy, provider routing, fallback text, non-Telegram channel behavior, public config, schema, migration, or protocol behavior changed.

Fixes #87299

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram direct-session users should receive the final answer without an extra generic failure fallback afterward.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 24.15.0, local OpenClaw dev gateway, worktree SHA `a5505616a4`, Telegram bot `@moongpbot`, model `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  TOKEN=$(security find-generic-password -a openclaw-daily -s openclaw-telegram-bot-token -w)
  DEEPSEEK_KEY=$(security find-generic-password -a openclaw-daily -s openclaw-deepseek-api-key -w)
  export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN="$TOKEN" DEEPSEEK_API_KEY="$DEEPSEEK_KEY"
  export OPENCLAW_HOME="/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87299-evidence/home"
  export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87299-evidence/state"
  export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87299-evidence/state/openclaw.json"
  export OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BONJOUR=1 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19072
  node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway
  # Sent Telegram DM to @moongpbot: please reply with exactly: openclaw proof ok
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
  pnpm check:test-types
  pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
  npx oxlint --deny=warn --ignore-path=.oxlintignore extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted runtime log excerpt from the patched gateway and live Telegram DM:
  ```text
  2026-06-04T02:09:10.779+08:00 [gateway] agent model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash (thinking=high, fast=off)
  2026-06-04T02:09:10.779+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: telegram; 1.5s)
  2026-06-04T02:09:10.880+08:00 [gateway] gateway ready
  2026-06-04T02:09:12.421+08:00 [telegram] [default] starting provider (@moongpbot)
  2026-06-04T02:09:13.820+08:00 [telegram][diag] isolated polling ingress started spool=/Users/zhangguiping/daily_pr_work/proofs/openclaw-issue-87299-evidence/state/telegram/ingress-spool-default
  2026-06-04T02:10:08.951+08:00 [telegram] Inbound message telegram:8903163841 -> @moongpbot (direct, 45 chars)
  2026-06-04T02:10:12.516+08:00 [telegram/send] telegram outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=8903163841 messageId=16 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text chunkCount=1
  Telegram observed reply: openclaw proof ok
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched gateway started with Telegram enabled and DeepSeek as the model, accepted the live Telegram direct message, sent exactly the requested final reply `openclaw proof ok`, and no generic failure fallback was observed after that final reply.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for live successful Telegram direct-message delivery; the artificial late post-answer dispatcher warning is covered by the regression test because it is not naturally triggerable from the normal Telegram DM UI.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus live Telegram smoke proof
- Target test file: `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: A final Telegram reply is already delivered or queued and a later dispatcher warning/error occurs; the chat must not receive the generic error fallback, while no-final-response failures still keep the fallback path.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test sits at the Telegram dispatch boundary that owns fallback delivery and directly asserts the externally visible reply/status effects without changing provider, gateway, or unrelated channel behavior.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Telegram dispatch treated a later `dispatchError` as sufficient reason to send a visible failure fallback, even when the delivery state already showed that a final response had been delivered or queued.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The regression suite did not cover the delivered-final and queued-final cases where a later dispatcher warning must not produce a second user-visible generic error message.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
